unit_16 0% 正确率 0 复习 0 错题

unit_16

推荐练习默认不提示本单元词。需要复盘时可手动显示本单元词；提交后判分，也可放弃查看答案。

50 推荐题 423 全部题目 0 弱相关题 30% 推荐答案命中比例
#1 SE
Although legislators on both sides of the issues have repeatedly ______ a desire to find a middle ground, until now no acceptable compromise has been achieved.Although legislators on both sides of the issues have repeatedly ______ a desire to find a middle ground, until now no acceptable compromise has been achieved.
答案：B, C　proclaimed; professed
middle groundcompromise
middle ground: standard unit headword | compromise: standard unit headword
#2 TC TRIPLE
To pay for the extra spending under this international poverty plan, each American would have to contribute less than the cost of buying a premium cup of coffee once a week. But financial aid is not (i)______, and even if the funding recommended here were to (ii)______, the grandest objectives may well remain unfulfilled. Nonetheless, carefully targeted aid can reward responsible governments, (iii)______ individual initiative, and alleviate suffering. Many will think that’s worth a cup of coffee.To pay for the extra spending under this international poverty plan, each American would have to contribute less than the cost of buying a premium cup of coffee once a week. But financial aid is not (i)______, and even if the funding recommended here were to (ii)______, the grandest objectives may well remain unfulfilled. Nonetheless, carefully targeted aid can reward responsible governments, (iii)______ individual initiative, and alleviate suffering. Many will think that’s worth a cup of coffee.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, I　a panacea; materialize; encourage
panaceaobviaterecede
panacea: standard unit headword | obviate: standard unit headword | recede: standard unit headword
#3 TC SINGLE
Gravity is the __________ of the four fundamental forces existing in nature, the others being the electromagnetic force, and the strong and weak nuclear forces; yet over large distances it is gravity that dominates.Gravity is the __________ of the four fundamental forces existing in nature, the others being the electromagnetic force, and the strong and weak nuclear forces; yet over large distances it is gravity that dominates.
答案：D　least potent
gravitydominate
gravity: standard unit headword | dominate: standard unit headword
#4 TC DOUBLE
Orchestra players are not always the best judges of conductors. Not surprisingly, when evaluating conductors they put a high premium on musicianship and (i) __________ and therefore typically gravitate toward conductors who are both technically competent and (ii) __________. But the ability to galvanize a hundred musicians into giving a great performance requires more than proficiency and affability.Orchestra players are not always the best judges of conductors. Not surprisingly, when evaluating conductors they put a high premium on musicianship and (i) __________ and therefore typically gravitate toward conductors who are both technically competent and (ii) __________. But the ability to galvanize a hundred musicians into giving a great performance requires more than proficiency and affability.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　collegiality; personally likable
gravitategalvanize
gravitate: standard unit headword | galvanize: standard unit headword
#5 TC SINGLE
In Protoscience times (in the ancient Greece), claims about the physical world were often accepted as true if they are reasonable; experimental verification, if thought necessary at all, was ______.In Protoscience times (in the ancient Greece), claims about the physical world were often accepted as true if they are reasonable; experimental verification, if thought necessary at all, was ______.
答案：C　perfunctory
egregiousempirical
egregious: standard unit headword | empirical: standard unit headword

Their forms thus necessarily become (i)______ over time and their original features are (ii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　degraded; obscured

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　surfeit; virtue; forgo

Blank 3

Laws protecting intellectual property are intended to stimulate creativity, yet some forms of creative work have never enjoyed legal protection—a situation that ought to be of great interest. If we see certain forms of creative endeavor (i)______ as a result of uncontrolled copying, we might decide to (ii)______ intellectual property law. Conversely, if unprotected creative work (iii)______ in the absence of legal rules against copying, we would do well to know how such flourishing is sustained.Laws protecting intellectual property are intended to stimulate creativity, yet some forms of creative work have never enjoyed legal protection—a situation that ought to be of great interest. If we see certain forms of creative endeavor (i)______ as a result of uncontrolled copying, we might decide to (ii)______ intellectual property law. Conversely, if unprotected creative work (iii)______ in the absence of legal rules against copying, we would do well to know how such flourishing is sustained.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　languishing; extend; manages to thrive
endeavor
endeavor: standard unit headword

The cowbird can seem a rather comical creature with a slow, awkward walk and often upraised tail. Less (i)______ is the cowbirds’ habit of laying their eggs in the nests of other birds. The (ii)______ nesters will usually accept the cowbird egg and raise the baby cowbird as their own. Less (i)______ is the cowbirds’ habit of laying their eggs in the nests of other birds. The (ii)______ nesters will usually accept the cowbird egg and raise the baby cowbird as their own. Unfortunately, cowbird eggs hatch sooner than the eggs of other species and the young cowbirds (iii)______, using their size to their advantage in getting more food from the parents.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　amusing; unwitting; grow quickly
unwitting
unwitting: standard unit headword

Blank 2

Only a few precious mementos were preserved and passed along. Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　discarded; immortality; death and decay

Blank 2
答案：A, E　swagger; pugnacious
chic

#51 TC TRIPLE
One sometimes hears that Marco Polo introduced pasta to the western world, having encountered it in China. This durable myth, which (i)_______ that nothing should have been known of pasta in Italy until 1295, when Marco Polo returned from the Far East, can easily be (ii)_______ by pointing out that there are Italian references to pasta that (iii)______.One sometimes hears that Marco Polo introduced pasta to the western world, having encountered it in China. This durable myth, which (i)_______ that nothing should have been known of pasta in Italy until 1295, when Marco Polo returned from the Far East, can easily be (ii)_______ by pointing out that there are Italian references to pasta that (iii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　requires; debunked; predated that event
symbolize
symbolize: standard unit headword
#52 TC DOUBLE
One radical approach to literary translation is to (i) __________ creating a (ii) __________ reproduction of the original work and simply to compose a new work, but in a manner inspired by the older one.One radical approach to literary translation is to (i) __________ creating a (ii) __________ reproduction of the original work and simply to compose a new work, but in a manner inspired by the older one.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　despair of; faithful
faithful
faithful: standard unit headword
#53 SE
The prime minister’s spokesman faces a difficult task in defending his boss from what he sees as the (i) of a decidedly prejudiced press. To make his defense effectively he will certainly have to give specific examples of the media coverage he finds so (ii) .The prime minister’s spokesman faces a difficult task in defending his boss from what he sees as the (i) of a decidedly prejudiced press. To make his defense effectively he will certainly have to give specific examples of the media coverage he finds so (ii) .
答案：B, D　lowly calumnies; objectionable

By pointing out the self-serving nature of the governor’s motives for supporting the new health care policy, the columnist implied that the governor’s idealistic-sounding explanation of her position on the issue was almost certainly ______.By pointing out the self-serving nature of the governor’s motives for supporting the new health care policy, the columnist implied that the governor’s idealistic-sounding explanation of her position on the issue was almost certainly ______.
答案：D　disingenuous
impractical
impractical: standard unit headword
#55 TC DOUBLE
The description of humans as having an internal clock is not a (i)______. Or rather, it is–you do not have a tiny watch in your cerebellum–but it also refers to (ii)______, a specialized bundle of cells that regulates cyclical processes.The description of humans as having an internal clock is not a (i)______. Or rather, it is–you do not have a tiny watch in your cerebellum–but it also refers to (ii)______, a specialized bundle of cells that regulates cyclical processes.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　metaphor; a real biological feature

In fact, they noted that more exercise often (iii)______ school performance, despite the time it took away from reading, writing, and arithmetic.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　physical activity; detract from; augmented

答案：A, F　pacific; dovish

But once the paradigm (ii)______, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)______ what has become the established view.

Blank 3

答案：D, F　realism; verisimilitude

external: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　arcane; obscure

Researchers must constantly convince administrators who control tax dollars, investors, and individual donors that the work they are doing will make a difference.Pioneering medical research scientists’ (i)______ claims regarding the (ii)______ new researches make the public wonder why their own doctors are not dispensing miracles. Researchers must constantly convince administrators who control tax dollars, investors, and individual donors that the work they are doing will make a difference.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　inflated; potential benefits of; oversell

external: standard unit headword

答案：B　malingerer

答案：C, D　brook; tolerate

Wolosky’s description (i)______ the poetry of Gilman, Howe, and Harper, but it is not entirely (ii)______ in the case of Wilcox, who hardly (iii)______ the materialism of her time. Rather, Wilcox seems to have embraced the amassing of private property.Wolosky claims that Ella Wheeler Wilcox joined other women poets such as Julia Ward Howe, Frances Harper, and Charlotte Perkins Gilman in critiquing materialism and possessive individualism. Wolosky’s description (i)______ the poetry of Gilman, Howe, and Harper, but it is not entirely (ii)______ in the case of Wilcox, who hardly (iii)______ the materialism of her time. Rather, Wilcox seems to have embraced the amassing of private property.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　fits; apt; impugned
impugn
impugn: standard unit headword

The genius of the scientific method is that it (i)______ the dictum of Aristotle that the goal of science is knowledge of the ultimate cause of things. True science, we now know, advances human knowledge by (ii)______ ultimate causes and focusing instead on the testing of empirical hypotheses.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　jettisons; ignoring

答案：B, D　averted; forestalled

But once the paradigm (ii)______, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)______ what has become the establish view.When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)______ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted toward positive results. But once the paradigm (ii)______, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)______ what has become the establish view.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　affirming; has become entrenched; undermine

Because the book is largely concerned with an examination of various (i)______ often encountered in contemporary thinking, such as an exaggerated appreciation for meaningless coincidence and a credulous acceptance of pseudosciences, much of the writing has a (ii)______ quality to it. Nevertheless, it avoids the overly earned scolding tone common to many such endeavors.Because the book is largely concerned with an examination of various (i)______ often encountered in contemporary thinking, such as an exaggerated appreciation for meaningless coincidence and a credulous acceptance of pseudosciences, much of the writing has a (ii)______ quality to it. 答案：C　recede

The process of copying manuscripts, the only way in which the fragile products of centuries of accumulating knowledge could be preserved, (iii)______, a situation that did not change for two and a half centuries, until the time of Charlemagne. In the intervening period, much of Classical literature was lost to us forever.For a time in the early Middle Ages, Latin culture came close to (i)______: the witness to that is the (ii)______ of manuscript copies of texts datable to the period. The process of copying manuscripts, the only way in which the fragile products of centuries of accumulating knowledge could be preserved, (iii)______, a situation that did not change for two and a half centuries, until the time of Charlemagne. In the intervening period, much of Classical literature was lost to us forever.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　vanishing; paucity; virtually came to a standstill

Blank 1

答案：C, E　transient; persistent

The growth of kelp in the kelp bed communities of the North Pacific Ocean is __________ sea otters: the otters prey on kelp-eating sea urchins, keeping the urchin population in check.The growth of kelp in the kelp bed communities of the North Pacific Ocean is __________ sea otters: the otters prey on kelp-eating sea urchins, keeping the urchin population in check.
答案：D　facilitated by

This scholarship was not illuminating the central themes of history but (ii) .
答案：C, D　synthesis; obscuring

#253 TC TRIPLE
Although Dostoyevsky was not the (i) __________ portrayed by some biographers, at least implicitly guilty of the terrible crimes he depicted in his novels, he was notoriously (ii) __________. Indeed, his attitude toward people who questioned him about his work was anything but friendly. No such problems bother students of Chekhov. On the contrary, it is difficult to think of a writer of equal fame and importance who, on close inspection, proves to be such a (iii) __________ human being.Although Dostoyevsky was not the (i) __________ portrayed by some biographers, at least implicitly guilty of the terrible crimes he depicted in his novels, he was notoriously (ii) __________. Indeed, his attitude toward people who questioned him about his work was anything but friendly. No such problems bother students of Chekhov. On the contrary, it is difficult to think of a writer of equal fame and importance who, on close inspection, proves to be such a (iii) __________ human being.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　monster; prickly; sympathetic
anything but
anything but: standard unit headword

Blank 2

Indeed, these studies indicated that the environmental (iii)______ of producing and recycling paper cups were similar to, if not more than, those related to the production, disposal, and recycling of polystyrene cups.Several studies (i)______ the assumption that paper cups, because they were made of natural products, were more environmentally (ii)______ than cups made of plastic (polystyrene). Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　dispelled; friendly; costs

#302 TC TRIPLE
Ironically, contemporary critics who celebrate the Victorian novel for its intellectual depth describe the (i) __________ effects of newer media - film, television, digital technology - in precisely the same terms that earlier critics had used to (ii) __________ the novel itself: as piecemeal entertainments that (iii) __________ mental effort on the part of the consumer.Ironically, contemporary critics who celebrate the Victorian novel for its intellectual depth describe the (i) __________ effects of newer media - film, television, digital technology - in precisely the same terms that earlier critics had used to (ii) __________ the novel itself: as piecemeal entertainments that (iii) __________ mental effort on the part of the consumer.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　numbing; lambast; obviate

#303 SE
Ultimately, all conservation is local and it is rarely practical, even if it were morally justifiable, to enforce conservation regulations in the face of widespread local __________.Ultimately, all conservation is local and it is rarely practical, even if it were morally justifiable, to enforce conservation regulations in the face of widespread local __________.
答案：C, D　antagonism; hostility
conservation
conservation: standard unit headword
#304 TC TRIPLE
Many researchers assume that politicians consider the preferences of the entire public, and not merely those of likely voters, when making decisions. Yet, since the desire to be reelected is central, it is plausible that politicians (i)______ the opinions of likely voters in formulating their positions on issues. It is possible that researchers’ suppositions about policy makers’ (ii)______ aggregate public opinion may serve to (iii)______ the fact that only the preferences of likely voters actually matter.Many researchers assume that politicians consider the preferences of the entire public, and not merely those of likely voters, when making decisions. Yet, since the desire to be reelected is central, it is plausible that politicians (i)______ the opinions of likely voters in formulating their positions on issues. It is possible that researchers’ suppositions about policy makers’ (ii)______ aggregate public opinion may serve to (iii)______ the fact that only the preferences of likely voters actually matter.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　attend disproportionately to; responsiveness to; mask
obviate
obviate: standard unit headword
#305 SE
Critics have raved about the beauty of Peck's prose, but in his fiction, at least, the diction has only gotten more portentous with time, the writing more overwrought, and the symbolism more __________.Critics have raved about the beauty of Peck's prose, but in his fiction, at least, the diction has only gotten more portentous with time, the writing more overwrought, and the symbolism more __________.
答案：C, E　ungainly; awkward

His importance as a critic of African America art and as an art theorist is (ii)______ if controversial, yet he has received (iii)______ attention for his unique insight into the broad forces that shaped American modernism and cultural nationalism in the visual arts.During the Harlem Renaissance, Alain Locke (i)______ the first flourishing of a self-consciously racial art movement in America and was widely credited with providing the philosophical basis for its emergence. However, establishing such validity, especially for a novel experimental finding, can take years, and what (ii)______ replication or extension may be (iii)______ for some time.Conventionally, the ultimate measure of a scientific work’s validity is how broadly and confidently its conclusions become accepted in the relevant field, which in turn (i)______ the extent to which its findings are replicated and extended. However, establishing such validity, especially for a novel experimental finding, can take years, and what (ii)______ replication or extension may be (iii)______ for some time.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　derives from; constitutes; disputed

The dust and rocks that rain down from space are indeed (ii) , even if some of the larger rocks are occasionally a (iii) . Matter trickles in, trickles out. But matter is not everything: an unending spate of pure luminous energy pours down on Earth in a 170,000-trillion-watt torrent.One of the great misconceptions regarding Earth is that it is (i) . This is true only if your sense of connection depends on large chunks of physical matter moving from place to place. The councilman was a highly respected, even (i) member of society, so when he was accused of fraud, people were (i) . (GEARS: SKBEIMIGRE]The councilman was a highly respected, even (i) member of society, so when he was accused of fraud, people were (i) . (GEARS: SKBEIMIGRE]
答案：A, D　venerated; stunned

#391 SE

答案：A, D　dismiss; skeptical

decipher: standard unit headword
#392 SE

[@M(SARS: sk#EITIGRE]Managers who categorically squelch insights from low-tiered employees run the obvious hazard of (i) creativity; conversely, these very same managers are more likely to (ii) any ideas that flow down from the top brass. [@M(SARS: sk#EITIGRE]
答案：B, D　smothering; unquestioningly embrace

foment: standard unit headword
#393 SE

答案：B, D　dubious; demur

acquiesce: standard unit headword
#394 SE

REARS: tkeZIIGRE)Federal efforts to regulate standards on educational achievements have been met by (i) from the states; local governments feel that government imposition represents an undue infringement on their (ii) . REARS: tkeZIIGRE)
答案：B, D　intransigence; autonomy

#395 SE
Up to the 1970s, histories of science tended to be (i) not least in their focus on discoveries and theories that could be read as anticipating later scientific orthodoxies, rather than on those deemed (ii) in their own periods. Historians of science are now routinely far more sensitiveUp to the 1970s, histories of science tended to be (i) not least in their focus on discoveries and theories that could be read as anticipating later scientific orthodoxies, rather than on those deemed (ii) in their own periods. Historians of science are now routinely far more sensitive
答案：A, D　anachronistic; major

fallacious: standard unit headword
#396 SE
The governor is known for her unwillingness to (i) policy decisions in the face of opponents typically, if she announces a specific policy and is confronted with objections from interest groups, she (ii) the policy. [(SARS: 3kRIIGRE)The governor is known for her unwillingness to (i) policy decisions in the face of opponents typically, if she announces a specific policy and is confronted with objections from interest groups, she (ii) the policy. [(SARS: 3kRIIGRE)
答案：A, F　adhere to; abandons

#397 SE
Motorcycle riders in London have discovered an ingenious, albeit___—_—s way off parking illegally without getting a ticket. [MUSARS: SkHEIPGRE)Motorcycle riders in London have discovered an ingenious, albeit___—_—s way off parking illegally without getting a ticket. [MUSARS: SkHEIPGRE)
答案：B, D　unscrupulous; dishonest

#398 SE
Early in the development of ice age theories, physicists identified the processing( slow gyration) and wobbling of Earth’s spin axis as the likely drivers of the ice ages, but geologists (i) , and even the painstaking work done in the 1920s and 1930s by Mihution Mplankovitch failed to (ii) of many of the day’s geological experts.Early in the development of ice age theories, physicists identified the processing( slow gyration) and wobbling of Earth’s spin axis as the likely drivers of the ice ages, but geologists (i) , and even the painstaking work done in the 1920s and 1930s by Mihution Mplankovitch failed to (ii) of many of the day’s geological experts.
答案：A, E　demurred; assuage the qualms

#399 SE
Song, speech, and music are typically one in the blues: the instrument complements the voice, and though they may play instrumental solos, the most__ blues artists sing through both voice and instrument. [#U{Song, speech, and music are typically one in the blues: the instrument complements the voice, and though they may play instrumental solos, the most__ blues artists sing through both voice and instrument. [#U{
答案：B, E　exemplary; representative
reminiscent
reminiscent: standard unit headword
#400 SE
The field of urban ecology is in ____________ phase: just a decade or two ago the idea of doing natural science in a city was something few researchers ever considered.The field of urban ecology is in ____________ phase: just a decade or two ago the idea of doing natural science in a city was something few researchers ever considered.
答案：B, C　an incipient; a nascent
obscure
obscure: standard unit headword
#401 SE
During sleep the brain-wave patterns of dogs are similar to people's, and they exhibit the stages of electrical activity that are observed in humans, so there is nothing there to __________ the notion that dogs dream.During sleep the brain-wave patterns of dogs are similar to people's, and they exhibit the stages of electrical activity that are observed in humans, so there is nothing there to __________ the notion that dogs dream.
答案：C, F　undermine; contradict
dilute
dilute: standard unit headword
#402 SE
Though Marie Cool and Fabio Balducci consider themselves visual artists rather than performance artists, their work seems __________, not firmly situated in either world.Though Marie Cool and Fabio Balducci consider themselves visual artists rather than performance artists, their work seems __________, not firmly situated in either world.
答案：B, E　liminal; intermediate
provisional
provisional: standard unit headword
#403 SE
The idea that Antarctica's subglacial lakes are ____________ has changed radically thanks to repeat-pass satellite altimetry technology, which has found evidence of water transfer between lakes at distances of more than 200 kilometers.The idea that Antarctica's subglacial lakes are ____________ has changed radically thanks to repeat-pass satellite altimetry technology, which has found evidence of water transfer between lakes at distances of more than 200 kilometers.
答案：C, D　isolated; discrete
obscure
obscure: standard unit headword
#404 SE
Some research suggests that praising children's talents and abilities may __________ their confidence, making them reluctant to tackle more difficult challenges that carry a greater risk of failure.Some research suggests that praising children's talents and abilities may __________ their confidence, making them reluctant to tackle more difficult challenges that carry a greater risk of failure.
答案：B, F　undermine; sap
obscure
obscure: standard unit headword
#405 SE
Because urban soils are formed from diverse, often non-soil, materials and have gone through variable periods of development, the normal heterogeneity encountered in wetland soils may be greatly __________ in urban wetlands.Because urban soils are formed from diverse, often non-soil, materials and have gone through variable periods of development, the normal heterogeneity encountered in wetland soils may be greatly __________ in urban wetlands.
答案：E, F　magnified; intensified

curtail: standard unit headword
#406 SE

答案：E, F　magnified; intensified

curtail: standard unit headword
#407 SE
The impact of the film on people's perceptions of what happened illustrates the power of fiction to __________ history: patently inaccurate, the story told by the film has nevertheless come to be the standard account.The impact of the film on people's perceptions of what happened illustrates the power of fiction to __________ history: patently inaccurate, the story told by the film has nevertheless come to be the standard account.
答案：A, D　trump; supplant
exploit
exploit: standard unit headword
#408 SE
Without a proper sense of its own legitimacy in Japanese society, the Liberal Democratic Party retreats to a sort of __________ politics that tries desperately not to offend anyone.Without a proper sense of its own legitimacy in Japanese society, the Liberal Democratic Party retreats to a sort of __________ politics that tries desperately not to offend anyone.
答案：B, C　timorous; craven
cantankerous
cantankerous: standard unit headword
#409 SE
Unlike schools of European modernism, the United States Black Arts Movement of the 1960s did not __________ its purpose or aesthetic and thus produced a heterodox array of art ranging from abstraction to social realism.Unlike schools of European modernism, the United States Black Arts Movement of the 1960s did not __________ its purpose or aesthetic and thus produced a heterodox array of art ranging from abstraction to social realism.
答案：B, D　prescribe; codify
obscure
obscure: standard unit headword
#410 SE
In the eighteenth and nineteenth centuries, Native American nations were hardly __________ entities: they waxed and waned, with some gaining power and others losing it.In the eighteenth and nineteenth centuries, Native American nations were hardly __________ entities: they waxed and waned, with some gaining power and others losing it.
答案：B, E　static; unvarying
fragile
fragile: standard unit headword
#411 SE
__________ the idea that attention is a limited resource, scientists have repeatedly observed that drivers using mobile phones are slower to react and more apt to miss important details than are drivers focused solely on the road.__________ the idea that attention is a limited resource, scientists have repeatedly observed that drivers using mobile phones are slower to react and more apt to miss important details than are drivers focused solely on the road.
答案：B, D　Bolstering; Buttressing
obscure
obscure: standard unit headword
#412 SE
Isabel DiVanna suggests that the ability of scholars to impose on the poem the lessons they wish it to convey depends on its __________ nature.Isabel DiVanna suggests that the ability of scholars to impose on the poem the lessons they wish it to convey depends on its __________ nature.
答案：B, D　pliant; malleable
obscure
obscure: standard unit headword
#413 SE
The artist reworked certain of his etchings in order to make the scenes they depict more logical, yet, paradoxically, the pictorial space seems to become more __________ the more realistically it is defined.The artist reworked certain of his etchings in order to make the scenes they depict more logical, yet, paradoxically, the pictorial space seems to become more __________ the more realistically it is defined.
答案：D, F　fanciful; chimerical
austere
austere: standard unit headword
#414 SE
Padilla is a contrarian by nature, so it is no surprise that the clothes she designs have consistently __________ established fashion trends.Padilla is a contrarian by nature, so it is no surprise that the clothes she designs have consistently __________ established fashion trends.
答案：B, F　challenged; flouted
impede
impede: standard unit headword
#415 SE
Even if quantum logic has not been shown to be wrong, it has proved to be __________: it fails to link up in interesting ways with mainstream developments in mathematical physics.Even if quantum logic has not been shown to be wrong, it has proved to be __________: it fails to link up in interesting ways with mainstream developments in mathematical physics.
答案：C, D　sterile; unfruitful
fallacious
fallacious: standard unit headword
#416 SE
She objected to the language in the report, arguing that it portrayed the central problem as stable and capable of being defined, when it was in fact __________.She objected to the language in the report, arguing that it portrayed the central problem as stable and capable of being defined, when it was in fact __________.
答案：B, D　nebulous; amorphous
impracticable
impracticable: standard unit headword
#417 SE
Partisans of literary realism sometimes think that because fantastical narratives do not abide by the laws of reality, they __________ all rules, though, in fact, the logic of fairy tales is often relentlessly rigorous.Partisans of literary realism sometimes think that because fantastical narratives do not abide by the laws of reality, they __________ all rules, though, in fact, the logic of fairy tales is often relentlessly rigorous.
答案：B, D　abjure; repudiate
obviate
obviate: standard unit headword
#418 SE
The __________ response from her opponent indicated that the politician's comments had done little to diffuse the acrimony of their nine-month-long dispute.The __________ response from her opponent indicated that the politician's comments had done little to diffuse the acrimony of their nine-month-long dispute.
答案：B, D　scathing; vitriolic
provisional
provisional: standard unit headword
#419 SE
The nation's robust economic performance could be ______ by the persistent flaws in its economic institutions and structure that are the result of half-finished and misguided government policies.The nation's robust economic performance could be ______ by the persistent flaws in its economic institutions and structure that are the result of half-finished and misguided government policies.
答案：C, D　undermined; impaired

obscure: standard unit headword | robust: standard unit headword
#420 SE
Flash floods are common in desert regions and were widespread before the evolution of trees and woodland soils; in contrast, flash floods are ______ in woodlands, where floodwaters, impeded by trees, form ponds.Flash floods are common in desert regions and were widespread before the evolution of trees and woodland soils; in contrast, flash floods are ______ in woodlands, where floodwaters, impeded by trees, form ponds.
答案：E, F　mitigated; abated

impede: standard unit headword | obscure: standard unit headword

[EARS : sk#EIMGRE)The political upheaval caught most people by surprise: despite the ___ warnings of some commentators, it had never seemed that imminent. [EARS : sk#EIMGRE)
答案：B, C　prescient; prophetic

#422 SE
In reviewing cases decided by lower courts, Supreme Court justices search for precedents to justify their arguments. Reliance on precedent (i) judicial restraint: the precedent (ii) a judge’s ability to determine the outcome of a case in a way that he or she might choose if there were no precedent. [#@MU{In reviewing cases decided by lower courts, Supreme Court justices search for precedents to justify their arguments. Reliance on precedent (i) judicial restraint: the precedent (ii) a judge’s ability to determine the outcome of a case in a way that he or she might choose if there were no precedent. [#@MU{
答案：A, E　promotes; constrains

decided: standard unit headword | compromise: standard unit headword
#423 SE
There's __________ throughout the Broadway adaptation of The Color Purple that, while hardly true to the harrowing bleakness of the early chapters of Alice Walker's novel, does bring to mind the enjoyably hokey cinematic lavishness of the film version.There's __________ throughout the Broadway adaptation of The Color Purple that, while hardly true to the harrowing bleakness of the early chapters of Alice Walker's novel, does bring to mind the enjoyably hokey cinematic lavishness of the film version.
答案：B, D　a splendor; a sumptuousness
